observations.title: 現状コード前提の不正確さ: runPostMergeCleanup は git pull を行わない
observations.rationale: request.md の「現状コードの前提」に「runPostMergeCleanup が main の後片付け（pull 等）を行う」と記述されているが、実際の post-merge-cleanup.ts は git pull を行わない（worktree 削除・branch 削除・sidecar 削除のみ）。要件 2「merge 結果を反映した main 上で実行」を満たすには、設計ステップで git pull の挿入ポイントを明確にする必要がある。要件自体は正しく実装可能。

observations.title: merge SHA 取得: 現行アダプターは SHA を返さない
observations.rationale: escalation に「merge SHA での帰属」を含めることが要件だが、現行の mergePullRequest アダプター（src/adapter/github/github-client.ts）は { merged: boolean; message: string } のみ返し SHA を含まない。GitHub REST API の merge エンドポイント（200 OK）は sha フィールドを返すため、アダプター拡張で取得可能。設計ステップで対処できる実装レベルの課題。

findings.title: failedStep hardcodes '(main)' instead of dynamic baseBranch
findings.rationale: T-02 specifies failedStep as the literal string 'post-merge integrity check (main)'. For repos whose base branch is not 'main' (e.g. 'develop'), the escalation label will be misleading. recommendedAction already uses <baseBranch> dynamically, creating an inconsistency within the same escalation block.

findings.title: No explicit test task for config schema validation of archive.postMergeVerify
findings.rationale: T-01 lists acceptance criteria (non-array rejected, empty-string element rejected, absent key OK) but no test task explicitly covers these cases. They are exercised indirectly only if existing schema test infrastructure covers new fields automatically, which is not guaranteed.

observations.title: SHA race condition on concurrent merges is acknowledged and acceptable
observations.rationale: D3 notes that origin/<baseBranch> tip after fetch could point to a different concurrent merge's commit. The mitigation — reporting the SHA as 'the merge SHA at the time of verification' — is honest and sufficient for serial archive usage patterns.

observations.title: { ok: true } return on infrastructure failure is semantically correct given the warning
observations.rationale: D6's choice to return { ok: true } on fetch/worktree-add failure with a stderrWrite warning is correct: the caller sees 'no integrity failure detected' (true — the check did not execute), while the warning provides the honest 'not verified' signal. This satisfies the requirement to not block and not falsely claim pass.

message: spec-review iteration 1 completed with verdict: approved
